tool.name: prettyseq
tool.summary: Render nucleotide records with a paired translated text view

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `prettyseq` renders a deterministic text report that shows nucleotide rows and their translated amino-acid rows together. The EMBOSS-RS v1 surface is intentionally small: one chosen forward frame, stable plain-text layout, and no attempt to reproduce the full historical pretty-printing parameter surface.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one nucleotide input path plus optional `--frame <1|2|3>` and `--width <positive-count>`. Translation uses the shared standard genetic code, and trailing partial codons are ignored.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The primary payload is a stable text report. Each record is rendered with a header line, one `FRAME N` line, one or more nucleotide rows prefixed with `NT`, and the corresponding amino-acid rows prefixed with `AA`.

sections.title: Legacy Context
sections.content: This acceptance anchor keeps one historical-style `prettyseq` invocation in view and compares the EMBOSS-RS text-report payload against a committed expected output. The governed comparison validates layout stability for the frame-1 committed coding fixture.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs prettyseq`. Current validation covers deterministic frame-1 rendering, stable line layout, and clear rejection of unsupported frame or input combinations.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports forward frames only and emits a governed plain-text layout rather than attempting byte-for-byte parity with historical EMBOSS formatting or richer annotation-aware sequence displays.

artifacts.label: Pretty sequence nucleotide fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-sequence fixture used for deterministic prettyseq text rendering.

examples.title: Render a frame-1 nucleotide and amino-acid text report
examples.description: Renders the committed coding-sequence fixture with forward frame-1 translation and the governed EMBOSS-RS plain-text layout.

expected_outputs.label: Deterministic text report
expected_outputs.description: A stable text rendering containing nucleotide and amino-acid rows for the committed coding fixture.
